name: GPT4All
tagline: Run large language models privately on everyday laptops and desktops
seoTitle: GPT4All: Run Local LLMs Privately on Your Desktop
metaDescription: GPT4All runs large language models privately on everyday laptops and desktops. Free desktop app plus a Python client, no API calls or GPU required.
overview: GPT4All, from Nomic AI, lets you run large language models (LLMs) directly on everyday desktops and laptops. You download the application, pick a model, and chat with it locally. No API calls or GPUs are required, so your conversations stay on your own machine.

It ships as a desktop chat app for Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu, plus a Python client built around llama.cpp implementations. The app includes LocalDocs, a feature that lets you privately chat with your own files, and it can run a range of open model architectures.
features: Desktop chat app for Windows, Windows ARM, macOS, and Ubuntu
Runs models fully locally with no API calls or GPU needed
Python client that wraps llama.cpp for programmatic use
LocalDocs lets you chat privately with your own documents
Nomic Vulkan support for local GPU inference on NVIDIA and AMD cards
OpenAI-compatible HTTP endpoint for serving local models

gettingStarted.intro: The fastest way to try GPT4All is the desktop installer, but you can also use the Python client to run models from code.

steps.heading: Install the Python client
steps.body: Install the gpt4all package from PyPI to access LLMs from Python.

steps.heading: Load a model and chat
steps.body: Create a GPT4All object with a model file. The first run downloads the model, then you can generate text inside a chat session.
steps.code:
from gpt4all import GPT4All
model = GPT4All("Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct.Q4_0.gguf") # downloads / loads a 4.66GB LLM
with model.chat_session():
    print(model.generate("How can I run LLMs efficiently on my laptop?", max_tokens=1024))

steps.heading: Or download the desktop app
steps.body: Prefer a graphical chat window? Grab the installer for your platform from gpt4all.io (Windows, Windows ARM, macOS, or Ubuntu) and follow the quickstart guide in the documentation.

useCases: Chatting with an AI assistant offline, keeping all data on your own computer
Asking questions about your private files and notes with LocalDocs
Building local AI features in Python without sending data to a cloud API
Serving a local model through an OpenAI-compatible endpoint for apps and tools
